result:
The task is complete. I implemented the three missing operations in `CsvRow.cs`:

- **`EscapeField`** — returns the field verbatim unless `NeedsQuoting` holds, in which case it doubles internal quotes and wraps the result in double-quotes.
- **`JoinRow`** — escapes each field and joins with commas; `string.Join` over an empty list naturally yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** — a single-pass parser: a field starting with `"` is scanned to its closing un-doubled quote (doubled quotes become one literal quote, commas/CR/LF inside are content); otherwise the field runs to the next comma or end of line. After each comma the loop always parses one more field, so the empty line yields `[""]` and a trailing comma yields a final empty field.

The build succeeds with 0 warnings and 0 errors (one build cycle used). I verified the round-trip property by tracing the edge cases: embedded commas and quotes, CR/LF inside quoted fields, empty fields, and trailing commas all reproduce their inputs, and `JoinRow([])` → `""` → `[""]` matches the spec's stated exception.
